message:
refactor(bootstrap): rename fingerprint prefix SHA256 to BLAKE3 to match actual hash

GenesisState::fingerprint() was emitting strings prefixed with "SHA256:"
despite hashing with blake3 internally — a long-standing inaccuracy
where the human-readable prefix lied about the algorithm.

Changes:
- genesis.rs: format!("BLAKE3:{b64}") instead of format!("SHA256:{b64}");
  doc comment and test string updates to match
- cold_recovery.rs, error.rs, phase.rs: update test fixtures and
  assertions to match the new prefix (8, 9, and 6 replacements
  respectively)
- lib.rs: update crate-level doc-comment example

No behavioral change — only the printable prefix now accurately reflects
the blake3 hash function in use. All tests still verify the same fingerprint
bytes, just with truthful labeling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
